rules_applied.reason: Figure was CD34+ cell count (PD response), not PK. Motixafortide is SC peptide, not oral.
Figure was 1,2-GDN metabolite (not parent GTN). Stored as 'oral 6.5 mg' but real NTG is 0.4-0.8 mg sublingual. Triple contamination: wrong analyte, wrong dose, wrong route.
Real oxymetazoline is 0.05-0.2 mg intranasal (pediatric Kovanaze). Stored as 'oral 18 mg' is 100x dose error + wrong route. Auto-digitizer parsed text erroneously.
Oral naloxone bioavailability is ~2%; Cmax at 20 mg should be <1 ng/mL. Stored values 2-102 ng/mL impossible. Figure was simulated multi-dose oxycodone/naloxone SS.
Real nirmatrelvir 100 mg gives Cmax ~1000 ng/mL; stored 2.0 ng/mL is 500x off. Figure was DDI scatter plot (AUCR), not C-t profile.
Real rucaparib 600 mg gives Cmax ~1900 ng/mL; stored 4.3 ng/mL is 400x off. Figure was BID steady-state (flat profile from t=0, pre-dose baseline), not single dose.
Figure was dexamethasone (DEX) DDI victim, not rolapitant. Stored as rolapitant but analyte was DEX.
Specific entry with Cmax ~7.9 ng/mL at 300 mg oral — figure showed digoxin DDI. Digoxin Cmax values are typically <10 μg/L and this matches the mis-parsed digoxin data.
Figure was urinary excretion (Ae, CLR) TABLE, not plasma C-t. Specific entry at 100 mg Cmax 6912 is 6x real literature value.
